title: System-level superadmin bootstrap (env-driven, idempotent)
forbidden_scope: Do not modify getAssignments or getScope in authz.config.ts (team-scoped only).
Do not modify AuthService, JwtStrategy, schema.prisma, or any controller.
Do not add a Team membership for the bootstrap admin (global role suffices).
Do not log the bootstrap password at any level.
Do not throw on missing env vars (must be a silent no-op).
acceptance: API starts normally when DEVPILOT_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL / _PASSWORD are unset (no-op, debug log).
With env vars set, an idempotent upsert creates/promotes a User with role='admin' and a bcrypt-hashed password.
Login with the bootstrap credentials returns a JWT whose role claim is 'admin'.
GET /api/admin/stats and GET /api/resource-pools with that token return 200.
Restarting the API does not duplicate the bootstrap user.
Changing DEVPILOT_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD and restarting rotates the password (old fails, new works).
schema.roles in authz.config.ts declares an explicit 'admin' entry.
Password string never appears in logs (email may).

verification.command: cd apps/devpilot-api && pnpm test -- admin.service.spec && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3101/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"admin@devpilot.local","password":"DemoPass123!"}' | jq -r '.data.accessToken' | awk -F. '{print $2}' | base64 -d 2>/dev/null | jq .role
verification.expected: JWT role claim === "admin"; GET /api/admin/stats === 200; user count with bootstrap email === 1 across restarts.
